channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Trump fundraiser shares plans for proposed ‘Garden of Heroes,’ East Potomac Golf Course remake as takeover looms
item.description: A fundraising pitch obtained by The Washington Post touts plans, which have not been approved, to dramatically reshape parts of Washington’s waterfront.

item.title: Elon Musk went to court. The judge wasn’t amused.
item.description: Elon Musk’s outsize reputation, provocative remarks and his friction with a federal judge have dominated the trial in his case against ChatGPT-maker OpenAI.

item.title: No, Spirit won’t rebook your flight. What travelers need to know after the airline ceased operations.
item.description: Budget carrier Spirit, which shut down early Saturday morning, offered some answers on its website — and much of the news is bad for its customers.

item.title: This fragile document deserved a celebration. At 100, it got one.
item.description: How the Declaration of Independence became the hit of the 1876 Centennial in Philadelphia.

item.title: Residents of a small town, a mix of conservative and liberal, discuss what’s really ailing American politics
item.description: Ten people gathered for a discussion about the rural-urban divide. They came together on a surprising solution.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after White House rescue deal falls through
item.description: The airline said early Saturday it had “no choice” but to wind down operations immediately, dashing hopes of a last-minute financial lifeline.

item.title: Tennessee and Alabama move forward with efforts to redraw congressional maps, in hopes of gaining more Republican seats
item.description: Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey and Tennessee Gov. Bill Lee both called special sessions aimed at picking up more seats, as party leaders try to capitalize on a Supreme Court ruling this week that limited a key provision of the Voting Rights Act. Those actions, part of a gerrymandering war unprecedented in modern times, could yield Republicans an advantage in three to five new seats — one in Tennessee and one or two each in Louisiana and Alabama.

item.title: Appeals court limits abortion pill access nationwide, blocking delivery via telehealth providers and the mail
item.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the 5th Circuit has temporarily reinstated a requirement that abortion pills be picked up in person. The move, which abortion rights advocates argue will make it harder for women to access the commonly used abortion pill, stems from Louisiana’s lawsuit against the Food and Drug Administration that allowed patients to access the medicine through telehealth and mail.

item.title: Pentagon to withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany amid feud between President Trump and Chancellor Merz over war in Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump has in recent weeks verbally attacked several European allies, including Germany, for what he calls their inadequate support for the war. Chancellor Friedrich Merz has criticized the conflict, alleging in public comments that Washington was being “humiliated” by Iran.

item.title: Three students. Lots of offers. Which college should they choose?
item.description: The Washington Post asked more than a dozen admissions experts what’s most important when choosing a college. Can you guess which schools these three students chose (and why)?

item.title: Why right-wing influencers are suddenly praising Islam
item.description: Tucker Carlson and others conjure up an unexpected respect for a faith as an alternative to liberal modernity.

item.title: FDA expands access to promising drug for pancreatic cancer
item.description: More patients will be able to take the pill while it is under regulatory review. The agency said it recognizes the dire need for a drug to fight one of the deadliest cancers.

item.title: Emergency room visits for tick bites are at their highest levels in years
item.description: Hospitals are seeing an unusually early surge this spring, but there are easy ways to lower your risk of contracting tick-borne conditions like Lyme disease, Rocky Mountain spotted fever and alpha-gal syndrome.

item.title: Trump administration asked NFL’s Commanders to run historic D.C. golf course
item.description: With the president’s sights set on prime golf real estate, the Interior Department is seeking new partners to run Langston, the historic home of Black golf in D.C.

item.title: Ex-Florida congressman and Rubio ally convicted of secretly lobbying for Venezuela
item.description: Prosecutors had alleged that David Rivera had received a secret $50 million contract to influence the first Trump administration.

item.title: Trump’s allies float a specious theory about treaties
item.description: Trump’s allies float a specious theory about treaties

item.title: Trump said he would end her political career, but Democrats did her in
item.description: Liberals say they want a fighter. But Janet Mills, a popular governor who clashed with the president, failed to catch fire in Maine’s Senate race.

item.title: Pope Leo picks formerly undocumented immigrant to lead West Virginia Catholics
item.description: Evelio Menjivar-Ayala, currently auxiliary bishop for the Archdiocese of Washington, in 2023 became the country’s first Salvadoran bishop.

item.title: Microsoft, Amazon and Google reach deals with Pentagon to use their AI technology in classified computer networks
item.description: The agreements follow a bitter fight between officials and AI lab Anthropic over surveillance and autonomous weapons.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Take today’s news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: Trump’s fight with Iran is as unpopular as the Iraq and Vietnam wars, poll finds
item.description: A Post-ABC-Ipsos poll shows most Americans call the military action a mistake, even as Republicans remain strongly supportive. Many fear the conflict will lead to a recession.

item.title: Iran poll. Medicare portal. Midterm primaries. Border wall. Camp Mystic. College quiz. Cow burps. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Database in Medicare portal exposed health care providers’ Social Security numbers
item.description: The Trump administration accidentally exposed Social Security numbers of health care providers listed in a Medicare directory, The Washington Post found. The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services has taken down the publicly accessible database, and officials say they are addressing the problem.

item.title: Carbon pollution is making food less nutritious and risking health of billions
item.description: Surging concentrations of carbon in the atmosphere have produced potent changes in the way plants grow, draining the nutrients from food.

item.title: Louisiana officially suspends House primaries as GOP calls for redistricting Southern states after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Gov. Jeff Landry (R) issued an order pausing congressional primaries until a new map is approved. Redistricting could help the GOP gain one or two seats in the midterms.

item.title: Trump’s border wall expansion just bulldozed an ancient tribal site
item.description: Construction in the Arizona desert damaged an enormous Indigenous ground etching resembling a fish that is thought to be at least 1,000 years old.

item.title: The terrible Michael Jackson movie exposes a central cultural question
item.description: The terrible Michael Jackson movie exposes a central cultural question

item.title: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval of war
item.description: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that would otherwise expire Friday.

item.title: Camp Mystic withdraws license application, won’t reopen next month after last summer’s deadly flooding in Texas
item.description: The camp canceled its summer season after coming under intense pressure from families of children who died as well as state legislators.

item.title: House passes DHS funding bill, ending bulk of longest government shutdown in history
item.description: Just Wednesday, House Republican leaders were insisting that the Senate-passed bill had a technical problem that made it impossible for them to support it.

item.title: White House drops surgeon general pick Casey Means, announces new nominee
item.description: Means's nomination stalled as some Republicans questioned the health products entrepreneur's credentials. The new nominee, Trump's third pick for the role, is Nicole B. Saphier, a radiologist and Fox News contributor.

item.title: Brain scans reveal 3 ADHD subtypes, including a more extreme form
item.description: Researchers have identified a more severe presentation of the condition marked by emotional dysregulation.

item.title: How King Charles tamed Trump
item.description: The response to the monarch’s speech revealed a courteous America.

item.title: Izakaya Seki earns our food critic’s first 4-star review
item.description: With understated precision, the father-daughter duo behind this Japanese restaurant in D.C. has built an enduring revelation.

item.title: Americans rejects Trump’s ballroom by wide margin
item.description: The president’s planned 250-foot arch and a Treasury Department plan to put Trump’s signature on paper money are also unpopular, a Washington Post-ABC poll shows.

item.title: A biopic in today’s On the Record
item.description: A biopic in today’s On the Record

item.title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills (D) suspends Senate campaign to unseat Sen. Susan Collins (R). The race is critical to Senate control.
item.description: Graham Platner, an oyster farmer, is likely to win the Democratic primary.

item.title: Economy picked up in early 2026, but inflation jumped, too
item.description: The U.S. economy grew at a 2 percent pace in the first three months of the year, new government data shows.

item.title: Voting Rights Act. Pete Hegseth. Trump’s ballroom. Jerome Powell. Hidden hunger. ADHD types. Uber for hotels. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Oil prices hit four-year high with Trump and Iran locked in high-stakes standoff
item.description: As the U.S. blockade and Iranian mines and drones halt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. gas prices also reached a level not seen since just after Russia’s Ukraine invasion.

item.title: Louisiana plans to suspend House primaries to redraw congressional map following Supreme Court ruling on Voting Rights Act
item.description: Gov. Jeff Landry told Republican House candidates that he plans to suspend next month’s primaries so state lawmakers can pass a new congressional map in time for the November elections, two people with knowledge of the calls said. The high court ruling, which found that Louisiana unlawfully discriminated by race when it created a second majority-Black district under legal pressure, positions the GOP to gain one or two seats in the midterms.

item.title: A cheap drug used by longevity enthusiasts may have a surprising impact on exercise
item.description: Researchers anticipated rapamycin would enhance the effects of working out, while also initiating health improvements of its own, but that wasn’t the case.

item.title: Johnson quells House GOP revolt to advance controversial bill on surveillance without a warrant
item.description: The procedural vote on a measure to extend the surveillance program by three years overcame fierce resistance from a group of Republicans who argued that the controversial tool threatens Americans’ civil liberties. The surveillance program is set to expire Thursday.

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Trump effort to end temporary humanitarian protections for migrants
item.description: The conservative justices appeared sympathetic to the administration’s contention that it can cancel humanitarian protections for Haitian and Syrian nationals.

item.title: Jerome Powell says he will remain on Federal Reserve board after his term ends as chair, citing DOJ probe
item.description: The move would deny President Donald Trump the opportunity to fill another seat on the seven-member board. Powell’s term as chair expires on May 15, and he said he would step aside for Trump’s pick Kevin Warsh to lead the board, once Warsh is confirmed.

item.title: U.S. jury convicts Afghan man of terrorism but does not link him to deaths in 2021 Kabul airport attack
item.description: The jury found that Mohammad Sharifullah was guilty of conspiring to provide material support to the Islamic State-Khorasan terrorist group but that his offense did not result in the deaths at the Kabul airport’s Abbey Gate. Thirteen U.S. service members and about 170 Afghans were killed in the blast.

item.title: Divided Federal Reserve holds rates steady amid two months of rising energy prices
item.description: The meeting is likely to be Fed chief Jerome H. Powell’s last as chair.

item.title: Former FBI director James B. Comey appears in court on charges of threatening President Donald Trump
item.description: Comey has denied that a photo he posted of seashells spelling “86 47” was intended to threaten the president, and he has vowed to fight the case.

item.title: I can’t stand my husband’s movie-quoting tic. Carolyn Hax readers give advice.
item.description: I can’t stand my husband’s movie-quoting tic. Carolyn Hax readers give advice.
